access.accessMethod: General Corpus checkout with pinned CME submodule
access.entityLabel: General Corpus source XML for aha2706

entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aha2706
notes: The file is 435,678 bytes. Its two BODY divisions encode the romance and the comparison appendix; title-page and omitted-matter structures sit outside those two verbal divisions.
The CME repository root Unlicense explicitly calls its subject software; this record does not extend that wording automatically to the XML data.

rights.basis: The CME root Unlicense dedicates software, but its wording does not expressly identify XML data as licensed material. U-M's current public-domain assertion is recorded separately for the upstream encoding.
rights.component: Checked-in XML packaging and repository distribution
notes: Do not treat the software-focused root license as automatic permission for unrelated manuscript photographs or provider scans.

rights.basis: The current item page says the materials are in the public domain and links the No Copyright - United States rights statement.
rights.component: U-M CME digital text and item materials
notes: The legacy XML availability paragraph saying copyright may apply is preserved as historical metadata but does not override the current item-specific statement.

dateStatements.label: electronic publication and header creation
dateStatements.notes: The header states that it was created on 22 February 2006.

entities.description: A 2006 uncorrected OCR encoding at TEI in Libraries Level 1. It retains Wright's title-page matter, complete extant romance, and comparison appendix, omits the verbal contents of the preface and index, and links 256 source page images through PB scaffolding.
entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AHA2706.0001.001
notes: The current U-M page characterizes the materials as public domain and supplies a No Copyright - United States statement; the older XML availability paragraph is retained as a historical, less specific statement.
Encoding metrics are not silently converted into a reconstructed complete-work extent.

rights.basis: The current item page says the materials are in the public domain and links the No Copyright - United States rights statement.
rights.component: U-M CME digital text and item materials
notes: The legacy XML availability paragraph saying copyright may apply is preserved as historical metadata but does not override the current item-specific statement.

bibliographic.citation: William Aldis Wright, ed., Generydes: A Romance in Seven-Line Stanzas, Early English Text Society, Original Series 55 and 70 (London: published for the Early English Text Society by N. Trübner & Co., 1873–1878).
bibliographic.pages: x, 246 pages; issued in two parts
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 55 and 70
bibliographic.title: Generydes: A Romance in Seven-Line Stanzas

dateStatements.label: separate issues
dateStatements.value: Part I 1873; Part II and completed title page 1878

entities.description: The completed EETS edition comprising Original Series 55 (1873) and 70 (1878). The romance occupies printed pp. 1–223, comparison readings pp. 224–225, and the glossary pp. 226–246; the 1878 issue supplied the title page and preface for the completed publication.
entities.label: William Aldis Wright's two-part Generydes edition (1873–1878)
notes: The scanned copy preserves the 1873 Part I wrapper, the 1878 completed title page and preface, and the continuously paginated two-part text.
The title page's paper/about-1440 witness description conflicts with Wright's own vellum/mid-fifteenth-century preface and current Trinity parchment cataloging.

rights.basis: Both parts were published before 1929; the scan carrier and provider usage guidance are separate.
rights.component: Wright's 1873–1878 edition text and editorial matter

bibliographic.citation: William Aldis Wright, ed., Generydes: A Romance in Seven-Line Stanzas, Part I, Early English Text Society, Original Series 55 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1873).
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 55
bibliographic.title: Generydes: A Romance in Seven-Line Stanzas, Part I

entities.description: The first separately issued installment, preserving printed pp. 1–112 and romance lines 1–3524. Its surviving green wrapper identifies Part I and the 1873 imprint.
entities.label: Wright, Generydes, Part I, EETS Original Series 55 (1873)

rights.basis: Part I was published in 1873, before 1929; the scan carrier and provider usage guidance are separate.
rights.component: Wright's 1873 Part I edition text and editorial matter

bibliographic.citation: William Aldis Wright, ed., Generydes: A Romance in Seven-Line Stanzas, Part II, Early English Text Society, Original Series 70 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1878).
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 70
bibliographic.title: Generydes: A Romance in Seven-Line Stanzas, Part II

entities.description: The second separately issued installment. It supplied the completed title page and preface, continued the romance on printed pp. 113–223 and lines 3525–6995, then printed comparison readings on pp. 224–225 and the glossary on pp. 226–246.
entities.label: Wright, Generydes, Part II, EETS Original Series 70 (1878)

rights.basis: Part II was published in 1878, before 1929; the scan carrier and provider usage guidance are separate.
rights.component: Wright's 1878 Part II edition text and editorial matter

access.entityLabel: Trinity College Cambridge MS O.5.2, ff. 1r–37v

entities.description: The principal complete witness of the stanzaic Generydes, occupying the first 37 folios of a fifteenth-century parchment composite. One original leaf is absent between present ff. 24v and 25r, creating the 187-line lacuna after Wright line 4619.

holding.notes: Generydes is the first component; Lydgate's Troy Book begins at f. 38 and the Siege of Thebes at f. 191.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS O.5.2, ff. 1r–37v
notes: Current evidence supports principal complete witness, not only surviving material: Emmanuel MS 405 preserves nine damaged fragmentary folios and early printed fragments also survive.
Current Trinity material identification and direct images contradict the paper wording on Wright's title page.

physicalDescription.extent: Composite codex of 211 folios; Generydes ff. 1r–37v
physicalDescription.format: Original white-skin-over-boards binding; several clear hands; decorated double columns
notes: The current catalog reports double columns of 50 and 48 lines for the codex; Wright described an average of 45 lines per column in the Generydes component.
Wright argued from independent quire signatures and heraldic decoration that the Generydes portion was formerly separate.

rights.basis: The manuscript text and decoration are fifteenth century; Trinity's copyright and CC BY-NC assertion for modern photographs is separate.

access.accessMethod: Trinity manuscript viewer and IIIF manifest
access.entityLabel: Trinity complete IIIF sequence for MS O.5.2
access.format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and 430 JPEG images

localCopies.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS O.5.2 complete digital facsimile
notes: ZIP integrity, all 430 inventory SHA-256 values and byte counts, and JPEG decoding passed; a direct-I/O whole-file SHA-256 recheck also matched.
The bundle contains manifest.json, inventory.json, and 430 image members. Its exact-source inventory records the width-only IIIF request profile 1800, for every canvas.
All images decode: 330 are 1800 pixels wide and 100 are 1801 pixels wide, reflecting provider responses to the same 1800, request profile.
This is an exact hardlink reuse of the canonical CME00051 O.5.2 bundle; no duplicate 1,355,929,310-byte payload was stored.

workPortion.label: Generydes (stanzaic version)
locators: missing leaf physically absent between canvas 52 f. 24v and canvas 53 f. 25r
notes: The complete bundle retains covers, flyleaves, Troy Book, Siege of Thebes, and all other provider-exposed matter outside the target locus.

rights.basis: The exact Trinity catalog and IIIF manifest apply CC BY-NC 4.0 to the digital object.
notes: Whole-codex research caching is separate from redistribution. Commercial or profit-oriented publication requires permission beyond CC BY-NC.

entities.description: All 430 canvases exposed by Trinity, from front cover through back cover and including every leaf of the composite codex. Generydes occupies canvases 5–78, ff. 1r–37v.
entities.label: Trinity complete IIIF sequence for MS O.5.2
notes: Complete means the entire current provider canvas sequence, not preservation-master resolution.
The full bundle necessarily includes covers, flyleaves, Troy Book, Siege of Thebes, and other matter outside Generydes.

rights.basis: The exact Trinity catalog and IIIF manifest apply CC BY-NC 4.0 to the digital object.
notes: Whole-codex research caching is separate from redistribution. Commercial or profit-oriented publication requires permission beyond CC BY-NC.

access.accessMethod: Current DIMEV release and University of York romance database; quote Emmanuel College MS 405 for institutional follow-up
access.entityLabel: Emmanuel College Cambridge MS 405 Generydes fragments
notes: No public complete physical-object facsimile or direct current Emmanuel item record was identified. The nine damaged folios must not be replaced by a cropped edition excerpt when whole-object research access is sought.

dateStatements.label: manuscript production
dateStatements.value: last third of the fifteenth century

entities.description: Nine damaged folios written on both sides, preserving a fragmentary second manuscript witness to the stanzaic version. The witness was identified after Wright's edition and therefore is collateral to, not upstream of, his text.

holding.notes: A direct current Emmanuel item catalog URL and public whole-object facsimile were not identified in this review.

entities.label: Emmanuel College Cambridge MS 405 Generydes fragments

physicalDescription.extent: Nine damaged folios, written on both sides
notes: Frank Stubbings edited the fragments in 1993 at pp. 317–340 of Cambridge Bibliographical Society Transactions 10.

rights.basis: The manuscript fragments date to the last third of the fifteenth century; any institutional reproduction terms would be separate and remain unverified here.

access.accessMethod: University of York romance database census pending exact current Trinity fragment crosswalk
notes: No file was cached. Obtain exact current shelfmark and request the complete fragment object, including recto/verso and binding or mount context, before claiming image completeness.

entities.description: The earliest currently recorded printed edition of the stanzaic version, surviving as a three-leaf fragment of about 450 lines at Trinity College Cambridge. The surviving fragment does not prove that the original print was issued incomplete.
entities.label: Richard Pynson Generydes print, c. 1504

rights.basis: The print dates to about 1504; current repository images and reproduction terms would be separate.

access.accessMethod: University of York romance database census pending exact Huntington and Victoria object crosswalk
access.entityLabel: Wynkyn de Worde Generydes print, c. 1506
notes: No files were cached and no cropped fragment image is substituted for complete physical-object coverage.

entities.description: A fragmentarily surviving early printed edition of the stanzaic version, represented by four leaves at the Huntington Library and one leaf at the State Library of Victoria.
entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde Generydes print, c. 1506

physicalDescription.extent: Five surviving leaves in two present holdings

rights.basis: The print dates to about 1506; current repository images and reproduction terms would be separate.

access.accessMethod: University of York romance database census pending exact British Library object crosswalk
access.entityLabel: Wynkyn de Worde Generydes print, c. 1518
notes: No file was cached and no cropped fragment image is substituted for complete physical-object coverage.

entities.description: A fragmentarily surviving early printed edition of the stanzaic version recorded at the British Library.
entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde Generydes print, c. 1518

rights.basis: The print dates to about 1518; current repository images and reproduction terms would be separate.

entities.description: The conceptual seven-line rhyme-royal version, distinct from every physical witness, early print state, scholarly edition, encoding, and repository artifact. It has 6,995 extant edited lines in O.5.2 but an internal loss of 187 lines caused by a missing leaf.
entities.label: Anonymous Middle English Generydes (stanzaic version)

rights.basis: The medieval work predates modern copyright terms by centuries; later editorial contributions and digital reproductions are assessed separately.

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., A Royal Historie of the Excellent Knight Generides (Hertford: printed for Henry Hucks Gibbs by Stephen Austin for the Roxburghe Club, 1865; postscript dated 19 February 1866).
bibliographic.publisher: Printed for Henry Hucks Gibbs by Stephen Austin for the Roxburghe Club
bibliographic.title: A Royal Historie of the Excellent Knight Generides

entities.description: Furnivall's edition principally prints the distinct couplet version from the Helmingham/Tollemache manuscript. Its preface and 1866 postscript reproduce early-print fragments of the stanzaic tradition; those paratexts are the comparison intermediary Wright explicitly names on his pp. 224–225.
entities.label: Frederick J. Furnivall's A Royal Historie of the Excellent Knight Generides (1865, with 1866 postscript)
notes: The main continuous romance is not the cataloged stanzaic target.
The exact object-level crosswalk from Furnivall's fragment sources to the modern three-print census remains unresolved.

rights.basis: The edition and postscript were published in 1865–1866; scan-carrier terms remain separate.

localCopies.label: Complete scan of Furnivall's A Royal Historie of the Excellent Knight Generides
notes: The main continuous text is the distinct couplet version; fragment transcriptions in its preface/postscript are comparison context, not the cataloged O.5.2 representation, so no work_portion is assigned.
qpdf --check passed. This is an exact hardlink reuse of the canonical CME00043 cache object.

rights.basis: The underlying edition is public domain; no separate scan license is asserted in the inspected carrier evidence.

entities.description: A complete 380-page scan of the augmented bound carrier, including the main couplet edition, preliminary stanzaic fragments, postscript, glossary, index, and copy-specific matter.
entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Furnivall's A Royal Historie of the Excellent Knight Generides

rights.basis: The underlying edition is public domain; no separate scan license is asserted in the inspected carrier evidence.

localCopies.label: Complete DIMEV 2.0-alpha.1 release archive
notes: The archive is supporting catalog data, not a textual or image representation of the cataloged target, so no work_portion is assigned.
ZIP integrity passed and the release contains data, schemas, documentation, citation metadata, and its CC BY 4.0 license. This is an exact hardlink reuse of the canonical cached release.

access.provider: Linne R. Mooney, Daniel W. Mosser, Ian Cornelius, and Michael Johnston

rights.basis: The release archive and Zenodo metadata apply Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

entities.description: A versioned current census record for the 6,995-line rhyme-royal version, O.5.2, the Emmanuel fragments, two early-print fragment groupings, Wright's edition, and Stubbings's fragment edition. Record 111 separately identifies the couplet version.
entities.label: DIMEV 2.0-alpha.1 record 2557
notes: This is a prerelease dataset; claims are pinned to the cached release rather than generalized across future DIMEV states.

rights.basis: The release archive and Zenodo metadata apply Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

next_steps: Consult current comparative textual scholarship and Stubbings's Emmanuel fragment study.
Do not transfer the couplet prologue's French-to-Latin-at-Hertford claim to the stanzaic version without version-specific evidence.
Do not identify any surviving French, Latin, or English object as the physical exemplar without transmission evidence.
openQuestions.question: What demonstrable source relationship connects the stanzaic and couplet versions, and what source lies behind the stanzaic poem?

next_steps: Consult Stubbings 1993 and current ESTC and repository records for exact shelfmarks, leaf identities, and printer assignments.
Preserve Wright's appendix-to-Furnivall intermediary relation until direct fragment consultation and current object identity are proven.
Request complete fragment objects rather than cropped textual excerpts when image access is pursued.
openQuestions.question: How do Wright's Fenn and Trinity-binding fragments map object by object onto the modern Pynson c. 1504 and de Worde c. 1506/c. 1518 census?

next_steps: Search or enquire through Emmanuel College Library using MS 405 and Stubbings 1993.
Record the complete fragment object's mount, binding, adjacent material, recto/verso sequence, and image terms before caching.
Do not treat Stubbings's printed excerpt or a cropped folio selection as a complete physical-object facsimile.
openQuestions.question: What is the direct current Emmanuel College catalog record, exact physical context, and complete-image access route for MS 405's nine Generydes folios?

next_steps: Prefer current Trinity material identification and modern romance-database dating for discovery while retaining all historical assertions with attribution.
Consult recent codicological and paleographical work before assigning a narrower production range or hand count.
Keep witness production distinct from the Midlands work-composition claim.
openQuestions.question: Can O.5.2's historical paper/about-1440 title wording, Wright's vellum/mid-fifteenth-century preface, current Trinity parchment/fifteenth-century catalog, and modern c. 1470 dating be reconciled more precisely?

reviewNotes: O.5.2 is the principal complete witness, not the only surviving material for the stanzaic version.

Wright's 1873 Part I and 1878 Part II are modeled separately within the completed edition because they were independently numbered EETS issues; each part also has its own pre-1929 public-domain rights assertion.
The cached Trinity object is a complete provider sequence; the work occupies only canvases 5–78 and one leaf is physically absent between canvases 52 and 53.
DIMEV, Internet Archive metadata/OCR, and the Furnivall comparison carrier are supporting sources with target_work_presence absent and no work_portion.

lineage.summary: The checked-in aha2706 XML is a repository copy of University of Michigan CME item AHA2706.0001.001, a 2006 uncorrected-OCR/TEI Level 1 encoding derived from William Aldis Wright's two-part EETS edition of 1873 and 1878. The encoding retains title-page matter, the complete extant 6,995-line stanzaic romance on printed pp. 1–223, and Wright's 161-entry comparison appendix on pp. 224–225; it omits the verbal contents of the preface and glossary/index. Wright transcribed Trinity College Cambridge MS O.5.2, ff. 1r–37v, whose missing leaf after present f. 24v causes a 187-line gap after line 4619. His comparison appendix depends on fragment transcriptions in Furnivall's preface and 1866 postscript rather than proving direct consultation of every fragment object. Emmanuel College MS 405 and the early printed fragments are collateral witnesses, not retroactive sources of Wright's continuous text. Complete local caches preserve the 273-page Harvard/Google scan of Wright's bound edition and all 430 canvases of Trinity's O.5.2 IIIF sequence; exact source, rights, and target-presence layers remain separate.

catalogSummary.title: Generydes (stanzaic version)

catalogingSubject.label: Generydes (stanzaic version)
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The cataloged unit is the anonymous late-fourteenth-century Middle English stanzaic version of Generydes, represented principally by Trinity College Cambridge MS O.5.2, ff. 1r–37v, and fragmentarily by Emmanuel College MS 405 and early printed witnesses. It is not the independent 10,086-line couplet version in Morgan MS M.876, the whole composite Trinity codex, William Aldis Wright's two-part 1873–1878 edition as a publication carrier, Wright's comparison appendix, the University of Michigan encoding and linked page images, or the checked-in repository XML.

assertion.notes: This is the modern romance database's work-level date, not the date of either surviving manuscript or Wright's edition.
The production ranges overlap and current evidence does not establish which witness was copied first, so no single source_lineage_entity_id is assigned.

dateStatements.display: Attested in Trinity College MS O.5.2, c. 1470, and fragmentarily in Emmanuel College MS 405, last third of the fifteenth century

assertion.notes: Only a fragment survives; this does not prove that the original printed edition was itself fragmentary. Wright's complete scholarly edition was issued separately as EETS Original Series 55 and 70 in 1873 and 1878.

dateStatements.display: First known printed edition c. 1504, surviving in a three-leaf Richard Pynson fragment

extent.description: One cataloged verse work, represented together with two zero-item editorial/carrier parts: the retained source title-page matter and Wright's comparison appendix. The romance is 6,995 extant L elements in 1,000 LG containers: 998 seven-line groups, one six-line group at lines 4614–4619, and one three-line group at lines 4620–4622. A missing O.5.2 leaf between present ff. 24v and 25r caused a 187-line loss after line 4619; missing text is excluded from the extant total. The XML also has 256 distinct PB elements, 74 folio milestones from 1a through 37b, 221 NOTE1 elements, and two BODY DIV1 containers. The second DIV1 has 161 P elements from printed pp. 224–225; those comparison entries and title-page prose are modeled but do not increment the intellectual-work item or form totals. Omitted preface and index divisions contain page placeholders rather than their verbal text.

formStatements.description: A Middle English narrative poem in seven-line rhyme-royal stanzas, normally ababbcc in pentameter. The encoding has 1,000 LG containers and 6,995 L elements; two LG containers are truncated around the 187-line physical lacuna after line 4619.

assertion.notes: Modern English title-page wording, notes, and comparison apparatus belong to the edition layer and do not make the medieval romance multilingual.

notes: The medieval work, each physical witness or print state, Wright's two issue parts, later bound scans, U-M encoding, repository artifact, catalogs, and image bundles are separate descriptive layers.
O.5.2 is the principal complete witness, not the only surviving material for the stanzaic version.
The 6,995-line and 1,000-LG counts are reproducible encoding metrics; the 187 missing lines remain separate from extant extent.

Rights for medieval text, Wright and Furnivall editions, U-M encoding, repository XML, Internet Archive/Google scans, DIMEV data, and Trinity images are recorded separately in the linked lineage manifest.

next_steps: Consult current textual scholarship comparing both complete English versions and the Emmanuel and early-print fragments.
Do not transfer the couplet prologue's French-to-Latin-at-Hertford claim to the stanzaic version without version-specific evidence.
Keep any lost physical exemplar distinct from surviving witnesses and later comparison editions.
openQuestions.question: What demonstrable source relationship connects the stanzaic and couplet English versions, and what source lies behind the stanzaic poem?

next_steps: Consult Stubbings 1993 and current ESTC/repository records for exact fragment shelfmarks, leaf identities, and printer assignments.
Preserve Wright's comparison through Furnivall until direct object-level use is established.
Do not merge fragment holdings or infer a direct Wright-to-fragment collation edge from modern identifications.
openQuestions.question: How do Wright's Fenn and Trinity-binding fragment groups map object by object onto the modern c. 1504 Pynson and c. 1506/c. 1518 de Worde print census?

next_steps: Prefer current Trinity parchment cataloging and the modern c. 1470 romance-database date for discovery.
Retain Wright's vellum/mid-fifteenth-century preface observation and his contradictory paper/about-1440 title wording as attributed history.
Treat O.5.2 as the principal complete witness while acknowledging the later-discovered Emmanuel fragment.
openQuestions.question: Can the differing historical and current descriptions of O.5.2's material, date, hands, and uniqueness be reconciled more precisely?

assertion.notes: The modern romance database assigns the broader romance to the Midlands. This claim is kept distinct from O.5.2's copying and ownership history and from its current Cambridge location.
Wright says both authorship and source remain obscure. The inherited U-M AUTHOR field names Wright because he edited the source volume; it is not medieval authorship evidence.

responsibilities.agentName: Anonymous Middle English translator or adapter

assertion.notes: Wright inferred that the story was translated twice into English but found no French source. The distinct couplet version's prologue claims French and Latin intermediaries; that claim is not transferred to this stanzaic version as a resolved source chain.

responsibilities.display_name: Anonymous Middle English translator or adapter

notes: Wright usually printed O.5.2 as written, corrected some obvious sense-destroying errors, retained manuscript readings in footnotes or the glossary, and did not regularize every defective line.

scope.description: The 1873–1878 two-part edition and comparison appendix represented in the encoding

subjects.label: Kingship, succession, conquest, and political legitimacy

summaries.value: Generydes, the unacknowledged son of King Auferius of India and Sereyne of Syria, enters his father's service and is driven away after rejecting Queen Serenydes and being attacked by the traitor Amelok. In Persia he wins military renown and the love of Clarionas, defeats rival kings, rescues her in disguise, helps restore his father's kingdom, survives further separations and betrayals, and ultimately marries Clarionas and succeeds to rule. The romance joins dynastic restoration, courtly love, battlefield prowess, disguise and recognition, loyal friendship, and providential prediction.

This record describes only the stanzaic version. The independent couplet version in Morgan MS M.876 has a different 10,086-line text and source prologue. O.5.2 is the principal complete witness; the nine damaged Emmanuel folios and three early printed editions survive fragmentarily and do not become sources of Wright's continuous text. Wright's printed comparison appendix is encoded but remains modern apparatus outside the romance.

The romance has 6,995 extant edited and encoded lines in 1,000 XML LG containers. Of those containers, 998 have seven lines, one has six lines, and one has three lines; the two short groups bracket the physical loss of one O.5.2 leaf and 187 lines after editorial line 4619. The missing lines are not included in the 6,995 total and are not reconstructed numerically. Wright summarized the missing episode from the other English version in his omitted preface rather than inserting that prose into the poem.

The checked-in XML retains Wright's title-page matter, the complete extant romance on printed pp. 1–223, and 161 prose entries from the comparison appendix on pp. 224–225. It omits the verbal contents of the preface and glossary/index while retaining their page-break scaffolding. The encoded comparison entries reproduce readings from printed fragments mediated through Furnivall's preface and postscript; they are not additional stanzas of the cataloged work.

assertion.notes: The parenthetical qualifier distinguishes this seven-line rhyme-royal text from the independent couplet version.

titles.supplied_by: General Corpus from the edition spelling and modern version-level bibliography

assertion.notes: This is an edition title, not a manuscript rubric. The same title page inaccurately calls O.5.2 paper and dates it about 1440; current Trinity cataloging identifies parchment and the modern romance database dates the witness c. 1470.

titles.value: Generydes, a Romance in Seven-Line Stanzas
In olde Romans and storys as I rede, Of Inde Somtyme ther was a nobyll kyng
Explicit the boke of Generydes and of his faire lady Clarionas

work.summary: Generydes, the unacknowledged son of King Auferius of India and Sereyne of Syria, enters his father's service and is driven away after rejecting Queen Serenydes and being attacked by the traitor Amelok. In Persia he wins military renown and the love of Clarionas, defeats rival kings, rescues her in disguise, helps restore his father's kingdom, survives further separations and betrayals, and ultimately marries Clarionas and succeeds to rule. The romance joins dynastic restoration, courtly love, battlefield prowess, disguise and recognition, loyal friendship, and providential prediction.
